name: CAN Error Frame Selectors
title: CAN Error Frame Selectors
description: You can access information of the error frame object using the following selectors:

selectors.name: CAN
selectors.description: Transmission channel or channel through which the frame error has been received.Value range: 1..32

selectors.type: Transmission channel or channel through which the frame error has been received.Value range: 1..32
Message IdentifierThis value is only available for some CAN hardware.

selectors.name: DLC
selectors.description: Data Length CodeThis value is only available for some CAN hardware.

selectors.name: SOF
selectors.description: Start-of-Frame time stamp in ns.This value is only available for some CAN hardware.

selectors.name: ErrorPosition_Bit
selectors.description: Bit index where an error has been detected by the controller.This value is only available for some CAN hardware.

selectors.name: ErrorPosition_Time
selectors.description: Difference between the time stamp of the error frame and the start of frame.Unit: nanosecondsThis value is only available for some CAN hardware.

selectors.name: FDF
selectors.description: FD Format IndicatorThis value is only available for some CAN hardware and a specific driver.

selectors.name: BRS
selectors.description: CAN FD Bit Rate SwitchThis value is only available for some CAN hardware and a specific driver.

selectors.name: ESI
selectors.description: CAN FD Error State IndicatorThis value is only available for some CAN hardware and a specific driver.

selectors.name: Phase
selectors.description: CAN FD phase.This value is only available for some CAN hardware and a specific driver.
selectors.type: 0: Error occurred in arbitration phase1: Error occurred in data phase

selectors.name: RX NAK Error.Passive Error Flag, if Error Code is Ack Error.

selectors.description: TX NAK Error.Passive Error Flag, if Error Code is Ack Error.

selectors.name: not used
selectors.type: RX Error.Active Error Flag, if Error Code is Ack Error.

selectors.name: TX Error.Active Error Flag, if Error Code is Ack Error.

selectors.description: 1: The error frame was send by CANoe/CANalyzer, for example with output(errorFrame)

selectors.name: CtrlType
selectors.description:
CAN-controller type.The error code format depends on the type of CAN controller.
0: unknown1: SJA10002: CAN Core

selectors.name: Byte(x)
selectors.description: Message data byte (unsigned 8 bit); possible values for x*: 063

selectors.type: Message data word (unsigned 16 bit); possible values for x*: 062
Message data word (unsigned 32 bit); possible values for x*: 060
Message data word (unsigned 64 bit); possible values for x*: 056

selectors.name: Message data byte (signed 8 bit); possible values for x*: 063
int(x)
selectors.description: Message data word (signed 16 bit); possible values for x*: 062
selectors.type: The index is byte-oriented; for example, int(1) references to the data beginning at byte 1 and consists of byte 12 (16 bit).
Message data word (signed 32 bit); possible values for x*: 060
Message data word (signed 64 bit); possible values for x*: 056

selectors.name: Message data interpreted as IEEE float (32 bit); possible values for x*: 060
double(x)
selectors.description: Message data interpreted as IEEE double (64 bit); possible values for x*: 056

tables: Identification Note: The validity of ID and DLC depends on the error type and the error position!

Error Code Bit Interfaces with CAN Core Interfaces with SJA1000 0-4 not used Segment 5 Direction1: RX Direction1: RX 6-11 Error Code Error Code Value Description Value Description 0 Bit Error 0 Bit Error 1 Form Error 1 Form Error 2 Stuff Error 2 Stuff Error 3 Other Error 3 Other Error 4 CRC Error 5 Ack Del Error 6 not used 7 Ack Error 8 Overload Frame 9 Protocol Exception Event 12-13 Extended Information Extended Information Value Description Value Description 0 RX NAK Error.Passive Error Flag, if Error Code is Ack Error. 0 not used 1 TX NAK Error.Passive Error Flag, if Error Code is Ack Error. 1 not used 2 RX Error.Active Error Flag, if Error Code is Ack Error. 2 RX Error 3 TX Error.Active Error Flag, if Error Code is Ack Error. 3 TX Error 14 1: The error frame was send by CANoe/CANalyzer, for example with output(errorFrame) Not used 15 1: Protocol exception Not used

CAN-controller type.The error code format depends on the type of CAN controller. 0: unknown1: SJA10002: CAN Core
Message data byte (unsigned 8 bit); possible values for x*: 0…63
Message data word (unsigned 16 bit); possible values for x*: 0…62 The index is byte-oriented; for example, word(1) references to the data beginning at byte 1 and consists of byte 1…2 (16 bit).
Message data word (unsigned 32 bit); possible values for x*: 0…60 The index is byte-oriented; for example, dword(1) references to the data beginning at byte 1 and consists of byte 1…4 (32 bit).
Message data word (unsigned 64 bit); possible values for x*: 0…56
Message data byte (signed 8 bit); possible values for x*: 0…63
Message data word (signed 16 bit); possible values for x*: 0…62 The index is byte-oriented; for example, int(1) references to the data beginning at byte 1 and consists of byte 1…2 (16 bit).
Message data word (signed 32 bit); possible values for x*: 0…60 The index is byte-oriented; for example, long(1) references to the data beginning at byte 1 and consists of byte 1…4 (32 bit).
Message data word (signed 64 bit); possible values for x*: 0…56
Message data interpreted as IEEE float (32 bit); possible values for x*: 0…60
Message data interpreted as IEEE double (64 bit); possible values for x*: 0…56
